html:
Exclusive to:

This article is incomplete because of the evil sabotage by Maxwell....
Please help to improve the article, or discuss the issue with the community on the talk page according to the talk page etiquette or on our Discord.

The Lost Relics are Items exclusive to the Hamlet DLC. The Lost Idol, Lost Totem and Relic Fragment can be found in various locations while exploring the world, and can be sold for a Tenpiece Oinc. None of the relics stack in the player's Inventory. Lost Relics sink in water and become Sunken Object.

Lost Idol, Lost Totem and Relic Fragment are renewable since Peculiar Object are renewable as well. However, Blue Sow, Jeweled Truffle, Royal Crown and Royal Scepter are irreplaceable.

The Blue Sow and The Jewelled Truffle can only be found in a room of an Ancient Pig Ruins that leads to another island, found in the ruins to the third and second islands respectively. The room will be filled with pressure plates that trigger dart traps, being fully illuminated during the day. The Regal Scepter can be found on the BFB island, and the Royal Crown is dropped by the Queen Womant (returning the crown to Queen Malfalfa will unlock Wilba for players who have not yet unlocked her). Attempting to sell any of these relics to any trader other than the Pig Queen will result in rejection and one of the following comments:

  • NO 'TIS PRICELESS!
  • IT IS FOUND! MUST FIND QUEEN
  • I CANNOT TAKE, BELONG TO ROYALTY

A player can give any and all of them to the Queen for a Royal Gallery Key, which will allow players to take one rare item (the Can of Silly String, which can be used to make the Skyworthy, the Key to the City, which allows players to build their own Hamlet structures, or Executive Hammer, which is the only way to destroy Hamlet structures).

Can be found Can be sold to
  • Directly on the ground in the Deep Rainforest Biome.
  • Attached to a statue (obtainable with ).
    • Tempting Idol
    • Erroding Totem
    • Crumbling Visage
  • Inside a Peculiar Object (examined with ).
  • As a Sunken Object in the Lily Pond Biome ( or ).
  • Rarely under Stone Slabs in the Deep Rainforest Biome.
  • On the top of some statues in the Ancient Pig Ruins.
  • The Professor at the
    Swinesbury Academy
  • The characters aforementioned,
    directly in the street.
  • The Pig Queen (only
    and , The Regal Scepter, and Royal Crown) at the Palace.

Lost Idol

The Lost Idol can be found atop a Tempting Idol statue. Its spawn code is "relic_1".

Lost Totem

The Lost Totem can be found atop an Eroding Totem. Its spawn code is "relic_2".

Relic Fragment

The Relic Fragment is the nose of a Crumbling Visage. Its spawn code is "relic_3".

The Blue Sow

The Blue Sow is a rare relic that can be exchanged for a Royal Gallery Key with the Pig Queen. It can be found in the Ancient Pig Ruins that leads to the Fountain of Youth.

Its spawn code is "relic_4".

The Jeweled Truffle

The Jeweled Truffle is a rare relic that can be exchanged for Royal Gallery Key with Pig Queen. The Jeweled Truffle can be found in Ancient Pig Ruins that leads to the Palace.

Its spawn code is "relic_5".

Trivia

  • Prior to the Aporkalypse update, the Blue Sow and Jeweled Truffle were unnamed relics that could be spawned in and traded with the Pig Collector for a Tenpiece Oinc.
  • Aside from the Pig Queen, Wilba, before being unlocked as a playable character, is the only other character that will accept both the Blue Sow and Jeweled Truffle; however, she will not reward the player for either relic.

Gallery

  • Example of bubbling animation above a Sunken Relic.
  • The two rare Lost Relics on the ground.
  • The Blue Sow as it appears on its pedestal.
  • The Jeweled Truffle as it appears on its pedestal.
  • Unused Silver Lost Idol

None of the relics stack in the player's Inventory. Lost Relics sink in water and become Sunken Object.

The Regal Scepter can be found on the BFB island, and the Royal Crown is dropped by the Queen Womant (returning the crown to Queen Malfalfa will unlock Wilba for players who have not yet unlocked her). Attempting to sell any of these relics to any trader other than the Pig Queen will result in rejection and one of the following comments:
NO 'TIS PRICELESS!
IT IS FOUND! MUST FIND QUEEN
I CANNOT TAKE, BELONG TO ROYALTY
A player can give any and all of them to the Queen for a Royal Gallery Key, which will allow players to take one rare item (the Can of Silly String, which can be used to make the Skyworthy, the Key to the City, which allows players to build their own Hamlet structures, or Executive Hammer, which is the only way to destroy Hamlet structures).

Lost Idol[edit | edit source]
“A priceless piece of history.”
–Wilson
“Its grin creeps me out.”
–Willow
“Is like little pig men.”
–Wolfgang
“Everything is lost to time, eventually.”
–Wendy
“ROCK PART”
–WX-78
“A priceless piece of Porcine history.”
–Wickerbottom
“This has been around awhile.”
–Woodie
“Someone might desire this.”
–Maxwell
“In theory this was a respectably jubilant fellow in the pig's history.”
–Wagstaff
“Surely it hölds great pöwer.”
–Wigfrid
“Wonder if someone would want this.”
–Webber
“I should return this to the pig-dudes.”
–Walani
“What strange textures.”
–Warly
“Long fergott'n treas're”
–Woodlegs
“A FELLOW OF INFINITE JEST”
–Wilba
“Old Twirly Tail thing”
–Wormwood
“Looks like something the pig history buffs would like.”
–Wheeler
The Lost Idol can be found atop a Tempting Idol statue. Its spawn code is "relic_1".
Lost Totem[edit | edit source]
“The relic of an ancient culture.”
–Wilson
“It's really old.”
–Willow
“Is thing from little pig men.”
–Wolfgang
“The remains of a long dead age...”
–Wendy
“ROCK PART”
–WX-78
“I must return it to the pigs.”
–Wickerbottom
“Hey there little buddy.”
–Woodie
“This might catch a pretty penny.”
–Maxwell
“Fascinating! Evidently, they had a runic writing system.”
–Wagstaff
“It must pössess incredible magics.”
–Wigfrid
“This is probably important to someone.”
–Webber
“Cool! What is it?”
–Walani
“An odd little fellow.”
–Warly
“'oary treas're!”
–Woodlegs
“WHENFORE THAT FROM?”
–Wilba
“Old Twirly Tail thing”
–Wormwood
“Pig history. Someone's bound to love this.”
–Wheeler
The Lost Totem can be found atop an Eroding Totem. Its spawn code is "relic_2".
Relic Fragment[edit | edit source]
“Looks valuable.”
–Wilson
“Snooty.”
–Willow
“Is stone piggie nose.”
–Wolfgang
“A long forgotten relic.”
–Wendy
“ROCK PART”
–WX-78
“This belongs in the hoofs of the culture from which it originated.”
–Wickerbottom
“Who nose what that is, eh?”
–Woodie
“Where's the rest of the face?”
–Maxwell
“Theoretically, it's a fragment of a larger sculpture.”
–Wagstaff
“A gift för the pigs, bestöwed by the göds.”
–Wigfrid
“Someone would treasure this.”
–Webber
“Someone would probably want this.”
–Walani
“I bet it picks up all sorts of aromas.”
–Warly
“Treasure b'this!”
–Woodlegs
“IS SPEAKS LESS THAN IT NOSE-EST”
–Wilba
“Old Twirly Tail thing”
–Wormwood
“This belongs in a museum.”
–Wheeler
The Relic Fragment is the nose of a Crumbling Visage. Its spawn code is "relic_3".
The Blue Sow[edit | edit source]
“A regal relic.”
–Wilson
“Looks fancier than those other old things.”
–Willow
“Is not mighty. Is fancy.”
–Wolfgang
“Looks like someone who died long ago.”
–Wendy
“ROCK OF SOME VALUE”
–WX-78
“Looks distinctly regal.”
–Wickerbottom
“Must have been some important lady.”
–Woodie
“Looks regal.”
–Maxwell
“A stone representation of some sort of royalty. Quite an unusual color too!”
–Wagstaff
“Decöration fit för Hela.”
–Wigfrid
“Looks like it belongs to a Queen or something.”
–Webber
“Woah. Looks fancy.”
–Walani
“This would make a pretty centerpiece.”
–Warly
“A queenly tre'sure.”
–Woodlegs
“IT LOOK'ETH LIKE MAMA”
–Wilba
“Ohh... pretty rock”
–Wormwood
“Looks fancy. And royal. And it looks like a pig.”
–Wheeler
The Blue Sow is a rare relic that can be exchanged for a Royal Gallery Key with the Pig Queen. It can be found in the Ancient Pig Ruins that leads to the Fountain of Youth.
Its spawn code is "relic_4".
The Jeweled Truffle[edit | edit source]
“It's an old fancy feast.”
–Wilson
“Ugh. Can't get those jewels out.”
–Willow
“Has the shiny rocks in it.”
–Wolfgang
“Even this doesn't cheer me up.”
–Wendy
“ROCK OF SOME VALUE”
–WX-78
“Quite a unique specimen.”
–Wickerbottom
“Oh. This one looks valuable.”
–Woodie
“It's absurd what passes as valuable around here.”
–Maxwell
“The ornamentation of this ancient artifact suggests it's of enormous value.”
–Wagstaff
“Surely this treasure shall win me förtune.”
–Wigfrid
“Wow. This looks pretty valuable.”
–Webber
“Not your regular old relic.”
–Walani
“If only it were a real truffle.”
–Warly
“t'be worth more than'a pretty penny.”
–Woodlegs
“(SNIFF SNIFF) DOTH NAUGHT SMELLETH”
–Wilba
“Fancy old thing”
–Wormwood
“Well it's valuable to someone.”
–Wheeler
The Jeweled Truffle is a rare relic that can be exchanged for Royal Gallery Key with Pig Queen. The Jeweled Truffle can be found in Ancient Pig Ruins that leads to the Palace.
Its spawn code is "relic_5".
Trivia[edit | edit source]
Prior to the Aporkalypse update, the Blue Sow and Jeweled Truffle were unnamed relics that could be spawned in and traded with the Pig Collector for a Tenpiece Oinc.
Aside from the Pig Queen, Wilba, before being unlocked as a playable character, is the only other character that will accept both the Blue Sow and Jeweled Truffle; however, she will not reward the player for either relic.
Gallery[edit | edit source]
Example of bubbling animation above a Sunken Relic.
The two rare Lost Relics on the ground.
The Blue Sow as it appears on its pedestal.
The Jeweled Truffle as it appears on its pedestal.
Unused Silver Lost Idol

The Lost Idol, Lost Totem and Relic Fragment can be found in various locations while exploring the world, and can be sold for a [[Tenpiece Oinc]]. None of the relics stack in the player's [[Inventory]]. Lost Relics sink in water and become '''Sunken Object'''. However, Blue Sow, Jeweled Truffle, Royal Crown and Royal Scepter are irreplaceable. The Regal Scepter can be found on the BFB island, and the Royal Crown is dropped by the Queen Womant (returning the crown to Queen Malfalfa will unlock [[Wilba]] for players who have not yet unlocked her).
